titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 60

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=142 status=live modified=2018-01-31 16:29:10.868826+00 | translation moa=143 status=live modified=2016-06-07 14:48:57.105673+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis . Henricus Comes de hathodl’ Salutem .
Sciant omnes tam presentes quam futuri

me concessisse . ⁊ hac mea Carta confirmasse deo ⁊ Ecclesie sancti andree apostoli ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo ser
uientibus ⁊ seruituris . Ecclesiam de Dul cum capella . et terris . ⁊ cum omnibus ad eam iuste pertinenetibus . in liberam
⁊ perpetuam elemosinam . pro salute anime mee . ⁊ commitisse sponse mee . ⁊ pro animabus antecessorum ⁊ successorum
meorum . Quare uolo ut predicti Canonici predictam ecclesiam ⁊ omnia ad eam pertinentia ita libere
⁊ quiete teneant ⁊ possideant . sicut liberius ⁊ quietius aliqua elemosina tenetur ⁊ possidetur intoto regno
scotorum . Sicut in carta Malcolmi commitis patris mei continetur .

Testibus . Comitissa Magareta sponsa mea
Alexandro de Setona . Malisio Iudice . Colino nepote meo . Michaele camerario meo . Anegus clerico meo
Ricardo clerico domini Electi sancti Andree .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Henry Earl of Atholl sends greeting:
Let all people, present as well as future, know

that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and the church of St Andrew the apostle, and to the canons serving God in that place now and in the future, the church of Dul, with chapel, and lands, and with all things rightfully pertaining to it, in free and perpetual alms, for my own soul, and of my spouse the countess, and for the souls of my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid canons should hold and possess the aforesaid church and everything that pertains to it, as freely and quietly as any alms is freely and quietly held and possessed in the whole kingdom of the Scots, just as it is contained in the charter of Earl Malcolm my father.
As witnesses: Countess Margaret, my spouse; Alexander of Seton; Malise the judex; Colin my nephew; Michael my chamberlain; Angus my clerk; Richard, clerk of the lord bishop elect of St Andrews.
